name: nav-docs-section
role: Top-level scope for the entire docs site navigation. Combines a top-bar (logo, search, version selector, theme switcher, GitHub link) with a collapsible left sidebar (doc tree). Manages 4 states: default (sidebar expanded, top-bar visible), sidebar-collapsed (sidebar minimized to icons or hidden), mobile-drawer-open (sidebar slides in from left as overlay), search-open (Cmd+K palette is open, sidebar dimmed). Mobile breakpoint: sidebar collapses to drawer at <960px.

semanticStructure: <nav-docs-skip-link href='#main' /> — first focusable element, visually hidden until focused
<nav-docs-topbar> — top bar with logo, search, version, theme, GitHub

registers.with-sidebar: Top-bar + sidebar. The 80% choice. Sidebar takes 260px (default) or 220-320px (narrow/wide). Mobile drawer collapses below 960px.
registers.topbar-only: Just the top-bar. Used on docs landing pages or single-page docs where sidebar isn't useful.

stateModel.default: Sidebar expanded, top-bar visible. The 80% state.
stateModel.sidebar-collapsed: Sidebar collapsed to a thin column of icons only (~56px wide) — saves horizontal real estate for long-form content. Toggle via host JS or CSS.
stateModel.mobile-drawer-open: Below 960px viewport: sidebar slides in from left as a full-height overlay. Click outside or Escape closes. Body scroll locked. Focus trapped.
stateModel.search-open: Cmd+K palette is open (composing pattern-modal-dialog). Sidebar gets aria-hidden=true via inert; focus moves to palette input.

ariaPatterns.skipLink: <a href='#main' class='skip-link'> as the FIRST focusable element. Visually hidden until focused (clip: rect(0,0,0,0) by default; expands on focus-visible). WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks.
ariaPatterns.sidebarNavLandmark: <nav role='navigation' aria-label='Documentation'> on the sidebar. Distinguishes from top-bar nav.
ariaPatterns.mainAnchor: Skip-link points to id='main' on the page's main content wrapper (rendered by the consuming template, not by this pattern).

name: nav-docs-topbar
role: Top horizontal bar with logo, search trigger, version selector, theme switcher, GitHub link. Renders as <nav aria-label='Top'> within the parent header. Sticky-on-scroll. Mobile: hides the version selector and theme switcher behind an overflow menu; logo + search + GitHub remain visible.

meta.stickyBehaviour: position: sticky; top: 0. z-index above sidebar. Border-bottom appears on scroll past the top via host JS adding [data-scrolled] (or via CSS scroll-driven animations where supported).

name: nav-docs-brand
role: Brand wordmark in the top-bar. Same anatomy as pattern-nav-marketing's nav-brand. Includes the product name + optional 'Docs' subtitle ('Quoin Docs').

meta.ariaLabelDestination: aria-label='Quoin home' (not 'Quoin Docs') — the link goes to the main home, not docs root. If the link goes to /docs root, use aria-label='Quoin documentation home'.

name: nav-docs-search
role: Search trigger in the top-bar. Composes the same Cmd+K palette pattern as nav-app-chrome's app-global-search. Opens a pattern-modal-dialog command palette on click or keyboard shortcut.

meta.samePatternAsAppChromeSearch: Identical contract to nav-app-chrome's app-global-search. The palette dialog itself comes from pattern-modal-dialog (variant=command).

ariaPatterns.ariaHaspopupDialog: aria-haspopup='dialog' because the palette is a modal dialog, not a menu.

name: nav-docs-meta-actions
role: Right-side cluster in the top-bar: version selector (dropdown to switch doc versions), theme switcher (light/dark/auto), GitHub link with external arrow. Composes <action-button> from pattern-button-system. Mobile: collapses to a single overflow kebab menu.

meta.versionSelector: Dropdown button showing current version (e.g., 'v1.0'). On click, expands to a list of available versions. Uses aria-haspopup='listbox' + aria-expanded.
meta.themeSwitcher: Tri-state toggle (light / dark / auto). Auto follows system. Implemented as <button aria-pressed> or as a 3-way segmented control. Icon-only by default with aria-label.
meta.githubLink: External link to repo. rel='noopener noreferrer'. Shows GitHub icon + optional star count (host-provided dynamic data). Trailing ↗ glyph if including text.

ariaPatterns.groupRole: <div role='group' aria-label='Documentation actions'> wraps the cluster.
ariaPatterns.externalLink: GitHub link carries rel='noopener noreferrer' and aria-label noting external destination.

name: nav-docs-sidebar
role: Left sidebar containing the docs tree. Sticky to viewport (below the top-bar). Hosts multiple nav-docs-sidebar-section children. Mobile: slides in from left as a full-height drawer when mobile-toggle is activated. Drawer has aria-modal='true' + focus trap + Escape-to-close + scroll lock on body.

tokens.transition: transform var(--motion-normal) var(--ease-standard), opacity var(--motion-normal) var(--ease-standard)

meta.stickyToViewport: position: sticky; top: var(--topbar-height). Scrolls within its own scroll-container when content exceeds viewport height. Scrollbar styled to be subtle.
meta.mobileDrawer: At max-width:960px, sidebar uses position:fixed with translateX(-100%). When data-state='mobile-drawer-open' on parent section, translates to translateX(0). Backdrop overlay appears.

ariaPatterns.navLandmark: <nav role='navigation' aria-label='Documentation'> distinguishes from top-bar nav.
ariaPatterns.mobileDrawerModal: When open as mobile drawer, sidebar gets aria-modal='true' temporarily. Focus trapped inside. Escape closes.
ariaPatterns.currentSection: The currently-viewed section's heading is NOT marked aria-current; the active page link inside gets aria-current='page' instead.

name: nav-docs-sidebar-section
role: A collapsible section in the sidebar. Has a heading (small uppercase) + a list of nav-docs-sidebar-link children. Can be collapsed/expanded via the heading itself acting as a button (aria-expanded controlled).

meta.headingAsButton: When collapsible='true', the heading text is wrapped in a <button aria-expanded aria-controls> so keyboard users can collapse/expand. The button is styled to look like text. Chevron icon rotates 90° on collapse.

ariaPatterns.ariaExpanded: Heading button carries aria-expanded='true|false'. Toggled by user click or Enter/Space.
ariaPatterns.ariaControls: Button's aria-controls points to the <ul>'s id. AT can announce the connection.

name: nav-docs-sidebar-link
role: Single docs page link in the sidebar. Anchor element pointing to a docs page. Active page gets aria-current='page' + visible accent indicator (left border or background tint). Supports a small badge (e.g., 'NEW', 'BETA') as inline span.

ariaPatterns.ariaCurrentPage: Active page gets aria-current='page'. CSS adds left-edge accent indicator (2px) + bg tint.
ariaPatterns.badgeAriaLabel: Inline badges (NEW / BETA / DEPRECATED) get aria-label describing the badge meaning. The visual letters are aria-hidden.

name: nav-docs-mobile-toggle
role: Hamburger button visible only below 960px viewport. Toggles the mobile sidebar drawer. Same WAI-ARIA pattern as nav-marketing's nav-mobile-toggle: aria-expanded + aria-controls + state-paired aria-label.

meta.ariaLabelStatePair: aria-label switches between 'Open documentation menu' (when closed) and 'Close documentation menu' (when open).
meta.visibilityBreakpoint: Hidden via display:none at min-width:961px. Visible at max-width:960px.
meta.focusReturn: On close, return focus to the toggle button.

name: nav-docs-skip-link
role: REQUIRED accessibility skip-to-content link. Rendered as the FIRST focusable element in the nav-docs-section. Visually hidden until focused; expands on focus-visible. Points to id='main' (host page's main content wrapper). WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks compliance.

meta.visuallyHiddenUntilFocus: Default state: position:absolute, clip:rect(0,0,0,0), height:1px, width:1px, overflow:hidden. On :focus-visible: clip:auto, height:auto, width:auto, becomes visible at top-left.
meta.wcag241: WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks requires a mechanism to skip repeated content. The skip-link is the canonical implementation for docs sites where the sidebar tree is the 'repeated content'.
meta.mainAnchor: Points to id='main' — host's main content wrapper element. Host must provide id='main' on the appropriate landmark.
